content: Hi, I'm Jamie. I just got back from a trip to Costa Rica and I'm still on a high from the experience. I spent 7 days traveling through San Jose, Puerto Viejo, and Arenal.
Welcome back, Jamie! Costa Rica is such a beautiful country. What were some of the highlights of your trip?

Definitely the triathlon training I did in Puerto Viejo. The scenery was stunning and the people were so friendly. And I also got to visit some amazing national parks, which was really great for me as a nature lover.

That sounds amazing. Triathlon training can be such a great way to stay active and challenge yourself. And national parks are always a great place to connect with nature. Did you have a favorite part of the trip?

I think my favorite part was the salsa dancing I did in San Jose. I took a class and learned some new moves, which was really fun. And I also got to visit some amazing museums and galleries, which was really great for me as an architect.

That's terrific! Salsa dancing is such a great way to meet new people and have a good time. And museums and galleries can be such a great way to learn about a culture. Did you have a favorite city or town that you visited?
I think my favorite was Arenal. The hot springs were amazing and the people were so friendly. I ended up spending 2 days there and even got to visit the Arenal Volcano on February 22nd.

Arenal is a great destination. The hot springs and volcano are really unique attractions. And visiting the volcano must have been a powerful experience. How did you find the food in Costa Rica compared to what you're used to in the US?
I loved it. I'm a big fan of gallo pinto, and I got to try all sorts of local specialties. I even took a cooking class where I learned how to make traditional Costa Rican dishes like empanadas.
That sounds delicious. Cooking classes can be such a great way to learn about a culture. Did you have a favorite dish that you tried during your trip?

Definitely the empanadas. I've already made them a few times since I got back. And I'm thinking of entering a design competition in Pittsburgh next year, where I'll be designing a sustainable building.
That's great! It's always exciting to share your passions with others. Good luck with the competition, and I hope you get to share your love of design with the community.

Thanks, I'm looking forward to it. And I'm also thinking of starting a blog to write about my experiences as an architect and triathlete. Maybe I'll even get to write about my next big project, which I'm working on for June 2024.
That's a great idea, Jamie. I'm sure your readers will love hearing about your experiences and insights on sustainable design and triathlon training.
Yeah, I'm excited to share my passions with others. And I'm also looking forward to my next trip, which I'm planning for August 2024. I'll be traveling to Peru and hiking the Inca Trail.
